provenance_note: Compiled from the compiling model's training knowledge of publicly documented claims. E axes encode the world's documentation of each case; compilation_confidence encodes the compiler's recall reliability for names, eras, and details — these are deliberately distinct (Addendum A.1). Threshold for instances: name + principals + rough decade + phenomenology. refs only where citation confidence is high; no reconstructed patent numbers or citations. Obscure cases are included by design; candidates tier holds leads known only as recurring mentions. Verify before citing.

instances.label: Soviet/Russian torsion-field program
instances.note: State-adjacent 'torsion generator' program; funding exposed ~1991; repeatedly targeted by the RAS pseudoscience commission (est. 1998, Kruglyakov).

instances.label: Filimonenko 'warm fusion' installations
instances.note: Soviet hydrolysis-energy claims decades before 1989; known mostly through later LENR-community retellings; program reportedly shut down in the 1960s.

instances.label: Kirlian corona-discharge photography
instances.note: 'Aura' imaging; corona discharge modulated by moisture/pressure is the established account. A migrated case like the radiometer.

instances.label: Tolchin inertioid devices
instances.note: Mechanical 'inertioids' claimed to translate without external reaction; friction rectification is the standard account; ancestor of the Dean-drive family.

instances.label: 'Gravitsapa' inertial engine satellite test
instances.note: Reactionless-thruster test reported aboard a Russian smallsat; publicly criticized by RAS members; no confirmed orbit change attributable to the device.

instances.label: Veinik 'chronal field' experiments
instances.note: Academician-thermodynamicist turned heterodox; clock-rate and weight claims framed in his own 'chronal' theory.

instances.label: Marinov absolute-motion experiments
instances.note: 'Coupled shutters/mirrors' apparatus claiming measurable aether drift; self-published corpus; died 1997.

instances.label: Mizuno excess-heat and transmutation reports
instances.note: Long-running electrolysis and glow-discharge program; book-length account published 1998; 2019 R20 reactor preprint circulated for community replication.

instances.label: Arata-Zhang gas-loading demonstration
instances.note: Public demonstration of D2 gas-loaded Pd nanopowder heat by a decorated materials scientist; helium claimed as product.

instances.label: Minato magnetic motor
instances.note: Patented pulsed magnet-wheel fans with claimed anomalous efficiency; media coverage, no independent efficiency audit published.

instances.label: Seike gravity-control claims
instances.note: Self-published 'ultra relativity' program; Moebius-coil devices with claimed weight and clock effects.

instances.label: Schappeller 'glowing magnetism' device
instances.note: 'Raumkraft' sphere device; never publicly demonstrated; survives via period accounts and later esoteric literature — the record indexes the period claim only.

instances.label: Reichenbach Odic-force observations
instances.note: Sensitives reporting glows and sensations from magnets/crystals in darkness; extensive self-published protocols; ancestor lineage of orgone-type claims.

instances.label: Mesmer animal-magnetism practice
instances.note: Baquet seances producing crises and cures; the claimed agent was a physical magnetic fluid.

instances.label: Royal commission examination of animal magnetism
instances.principals: Franklin, Lavoisier, Bailly, Guillotin et al.
instances.note: Blinded trials at Passy attributing effects to imagination and imitation — the earliest C4 adversarial examination in this corpus.

instances.label: Munich 'barn' dowsing experiments
instances.note: Large screened-dowser study; authors reported a few 'skilled' outliers under double-blind conditions.

instances.label: Enright reanalysis of the Munich data
instances.note: Statistical reanalysis finding results consistent with chance; selection of outliers as the artifact.

instances.label: Ehrenhaft subelectron charge claims
instances.note: Charges below e reported in oil-drop-type experiments against Millikan; resolved via droplet physics and selection analysis; opens the promotion counter on P-EMG-SON-01.

instances.label: Kowsky-Frost quartz levitation story
instances.note: Widely reprinted period story of an RF-expanded, load-lifting quartz crystal; commonly assessed as a magazine hoax; indexed as a print claim.

instances.label: Hubbard coil boat demonstration
instances.note: Press-covered 'fuelless' coil said to drive a boat; later accounts tie the design to concealed radium energy — indexed as period claim plus conventional account.

instances.label: Michelson-Morley aether-drift null
instances.note: Orthodox anchor: expected drift absent; the null that reoriented physics.

instances.label: Miller Mount Wilson aether-drift claims
instances.note: Persistent ~small drift signal claimed across thousands of turns.

instances.label: Shankland reanalysis of Miller data
instances.note: Signal attributed to thermal gradients and statistical treatment.

instances.label: Sagnac rotation interferometry
instances.note: Real, foundational effect originally interpreted by its discoverer as aether evidence — interpretation migrated, phenomenon stayed; today the basis of ring-laser gyros.

instances.label: Aspden rotor 'virtual inertia' anecdote
instances.note: Report that a restarted rotor briefly required anomalously little spin-up energy after a stop; bench anecdote, never systematized.

instances.label: Majorana gravitational-absorption experiments
instances.note: Published series claiming tiny weight reduction of a mass screened by mercury/lead; never reproduced; modern constraints exclude the claimed magnitude.

instances.label: Brush composition-dependent gravity claims
instances.note: Papers claiming certain complex silicates fall with slightly different acceleration; contradicted by the Eoetvoes tradition of composition-independence.

instances.label: Nipher electro-gravitation experiments
instances.note: Cavendish-type setup with electrified masses; claimed modification of gravitational attraction; electrostatic force on the apparatus is the standard account.

instances.label: Wallace 'kinemassic field' patents
instances.note: Patented claims of a gravitational-like field from spinning odd-isotope material; patent documents the claim, no published measurements.

instances.label: Kidd gyroscopic propulsion device
instances.note: Twin-gyro rig with claimed net lift; TV-era coverage and university bench visits; no controlled confirmation.

instances.label: Fifth-force reanalysis of Eoetvoes data
instances.note: Reanalysis suggesting composition-dependent coupling; triggered a worldwide measurement campaign incl. an early claimed positive (Thieberger float experiment).

instances.label: Eoet-Wash torsion-balance resolution
instances.note: High-precision nulls closed the fifth-force window; local mass gradients and data treatment explained earlier signals. Model resolution arc entirely inside orthodoxy.

instances.label: Patterson Power Cell demonstrations
instances.note: Coated-bead electrolytic cell shown at trade conferences with high claimed COP; kits circulated; never independently validated or commercialized.

instances.label: Klein HHO/Aquygen media wave
instances.note: TV-covered torch and car demonstrations under a branded oxyhydrogen; conventional combustion accounts discussed.

instances.label: Kanarev low-current electrolysis claims
instances.note: Pulsed 'plasma electrolysis' with claimed over-unity hydrogen yield; self-published monographs.

instances.label: Santilli magnecule gas claims
instances.note: Arc-gasification fuel marketed with claimed novel 'magnecule' species; conventional syngas chemistry is the discussed account.

instances.label: Puharich resonant water-splitting patent
instances.note: Patented claims of efficient resonant water dissociation; patent documents the claim.

instances.label: Abrams ERA radionics practice
instances.note: 'Electronic Reactions of Abrams': diagnosis from blood spots via box instruments; large practitioner network in the early 1920s.

instances.label: Scientific American ERA investigation
instances.note: Year-long blinded examination concluding the devices had no diagnostic value.

instances.label: Drown radio-vision instruments
instances.note: Claimed photographic imaging and treatment of distant patients from a blood sample. A 1949 University of Chicago test of the diagnostic device failed: three of the first blood specimens were so badly misread that the remaining seven were not attempted. Convicted in 1951 of introducing a misbranded device into interstate commerce and fined USD 1,000 — a labelling offence under food and drug law, not a fraud finding. A 1963 California grand-theft charge did not come to trial; she died in 1965.
refs: Ralph Lee Smith, 'The Incredible Drown Case', Today's Health (American Medical Association), April 1968 — reproduced at quackwatch.org/chiropractic/hx/drown/.

instances.label: de la Warr radionic camera
instances.note: UK radionics laboratory producing 'thought photographs'; 1960 civil litigation ended without a fraud finding.

instances.label: Hieronymus eloptic-energy analyzer
instances.note: Patented 'emanation' analyzer; Astounding editor J.W. Campbell reported symbolic circuit-diagram versions working equally well — inadvertently a suggestion-artifact experiment.

instances.label: Lakhovsky Multiple Wave Oscillator trials
instances.note: Plant-tumor and clinical claims around broadband spark-gap coils; hospital-adjacent trials reported in period sources.

instances.label: Priore plasma-tube therapy machine
instances.note: Large undisclosed-principle machine; animal-trial cures reported with Academy-level advocacy and state funding; device principle never disclosed, program collapsed.

instances.label: Perendev magnet motor venture
instances.note: Video-marketed all-magnet motor with investment scheme; the venture was the subject of subsequent legal proceedings. No court outcome is asserted here — none could be verified against a primary source.

instances.label: Yildiz magnet motor demonstration
instances.note: University-venue demonstration (Delft, 2010) of a self-running magnet motor; teardown under controls never granted.

instances.label: Heins 'Perepiteia' regenerative claims
instances.note: Motor acceleration under load claimed; press-reported bench visits at university labs; conventional back-EMF/field-weakening accounts discussed.

instances.label: Bhaskara overbalanced wheel description
instances.note: Earliest known perpetual-wheel description (mercury-filled spokes); the deep-history root of S-SLF; Villard de Honnecourt's 13th-c. sketch continues the lineage.

instances.label: Cox's barometric perpetual clock
instances.note: Orthodox anchor: an apparent self-runner with a fully documented energy source (barometric pressure); the control case every S-SLF claim is measured against.

instances.label: Blondlot N-rays
instances.note: New radiation reported via faint screen-brightness changes; dozens of confirming papers within France in one year — a replication bubble.

instances.label: Wood's examination of N-rays
instances.note: Covert prism removal left reported observations unchanged; the canonical observer-expectancy exposure.

instances.label: Polywater / anomalous water
instances.note: Capillary-condensed water with anomalous properties; a global replication wave followed.

instances.label: Polywater contamination resolution
instances.note: Silicate/organic contamination identified; original group conceded — a clean self-correction arc.

instances.label: Mpemba effect
instances.note: Hot-water-freezes-faster reports; multiple conventional mechanisms discussed (evaporation, convection, supercooling, definitions), none settled — a live orthodox-sector anomaly.

instances.label: Pioneer anomalous acceleration
instances.note: Sunward ~1e-9 m/s^2 residual on both spacecraft; open anomaly for 14 years.

instances.label: Pioneer thermal-recoil resolution
instances.note: Anisotropic thermal radiation recoil accounts for the acceleration — a migrated anomaly, radiometer-style, at spacecraft scale.

instances.label: OPERA superluminal-neutrino announcement
instances.note: 60 ns early neutrino arrival reported with explicit request for scrutiny.

instances.label: OPERA fiber/clock artifact resolution
instances.note: Loose fiber connector and clock drift identified; ICARUS null; textbook fast self-correction.

instances.label: NASA lattice-confinement fusion
instances.note: Gamma-driven deuteron fusion in metal lattices published in a mainstream venue — orthodox-adjacent boundary case for the S-LNR neighborhood.

instances.label: Piantelli-Focardi Ni-H anomalous heat
instances.note: Nickel-hydrogen excess-heat series published in Il Nuovo Cimento (1994); the direct ancestor of the E-Cat lineage.

instances.label: SPAWAR co-deposition CR-39 tracks
instances.note: Pd co-deposition with track-detector pits read as energetic particles; chemical-damage accounts discussed.

candidates.recall: E.W. Silvertooth, 1986, claimed standing-wave detection of absolute motion
candidates.verify: publication venue (Nature correspondence vs. Speculations Sci. Technol.) and apparatus details

candidates.recall: Yu. Galaev, 2000s, radio-band and optical drift-measurement claims, Kharkov
Gennady Dulnev, St. Petersburg ITMO, instrumented 'psi/torsion' heat-flux measurements, 1990s-2000s
candidates.verify: which reports exist beyond institutional lore

candidates.recall: Toyota Central R&D group reported partial replication of permeation transmutation ~2013 (Hioki et al., JJAP?)
Ryushin Ohmasa, vibration-stirred electrolysis gas with anomalous-property claims, Japan 2000s
Akito Takahashi, multi-body fusion models plus group experiments, 1990s-2010s
candidates.verify: which experimental records stand independent of theory papers

candidates.recall: Oskar Korschelt, Leipzig ~1890s, patented therapeutic 'aether ray' device; radionics ancestor
G.S. Piggott, ~1904, silver balls suspended near charged sphere, cited in electrogravitics lore
Pier Luigi Ighina, Italy, 'magnetic atom', rain/cloud apparatus, Elios machine; colorful primary interviews exist
candidates.verify: which discrete observations are documented vs. narrated

candidates.recall: Hans Nieper, Hannover, 1980s 'gravity field energy' conferences promoting converters (Kromrey, others)
Adam Trombly, 1980s, homopolar generator with over-unity claims and suppression lore
candidates.verify: patent application record and any measurements

candidates.recall: Donald L. Smith, resonant HV coil devices, videotaped talks 1990s-2000s
John Ecklin, stationary-armature generator concept, 1970s-80s newsletters
Raymond Kromrey, 1960s(?), G-field generator claims
William Hyde, high-efficiency electrostatic machine claims, patent often cited ~1990
candidates.verify: patent number and content before citing

candidates.recall: Stephen Horvath, Australia, 1970s-80s, hydrogen-from-water car claims with patents
2002-03 'Edison device' water-energy investment scheme; later fraud prosecution
candidates.verify: principal names and case record — deliberately not asserted from memory

candidates.recall: Rife-derived frequency devices, 1950s-60s, prosecution ~1961
Andre Bovis, 1930s France, dowsing-scale 'Bovis units' instrumentation
Robert Cook (CIP) and 'Thornson drive' as recurring reactionless-drive mentions, 1970s-80s

deferred_and_omitted.psi_class_reserved: Mental-influence cases (Kulagina, Geller, Serios, D.D. Home) are phenomenologically recordable but deferred until the reserved PSI class is activated — respecting the standard's own namespace discipline rather than forcing them into MEC/OPT.
deferred_and_omitted.env_class_reserved: Weather/geophysical claims (Reich cloudbuster, Ighina rain apparatus, hail cannons) deferred to the reserved ENV class.
deferred_and_omitted.theory_first_figures: Figures whose corpus is theory rather than discrete bench observations (Burkhard Heim, Nassim Haramein, Shipov-as-theorist) are out of scope by the observable-primacy principle; their experimental wings, where they exist, are recorded (Akimov program).
deferred_and_omitted.mythos_without_primary_documentation: Die Glocke and the Vril/Nazi-UFO complex are deliberately excluded: they originate in postwar and 2000s secondary literature with no primary observation documents. Schauberger and Schappeller are included only insofar as period claims exist.
deferred_and_omitted.doctrines: Cosmological doctrines without discrete observations (Welteislehre) are not observation records.
deferred_and_omitted.active_ventures: Present-day commercial ventures in active litigation/marketing flux (Brillouin, Orbo successors, ministry-style outfits) excluded from this release; verification churn too high.
deferred_and_omitted.active_orthodox_frontier: Live mainstream anomalies (DAMA/LIBRA modulation, muon g-2, Hubble tension) excluded not for obscurity but because their classification is pending and they are already systematically referenced elsewhere.
deferred_and_omitted.institutions: Institutions without discrete observations (Gravity Research Foundation, 1956 electrogravitics trade-press wave) appear only via attached observation records (Brown, Winterhaven).
